preamble: The shift from the alveolar fricative [s] to the postalveolar affricate [tʃ] is a form of palatalization and affrication. It typically occurs in rapid speech through yod-coalescence, where a word-final sibilant merges with a word-initial palatal glide [j] to create a complex affricate.

examples.note: In rapid or informal speech, the [s] and [j] coalesce into a postalveolar affricate.
Widespread palatalization of dental sibilants before front vowels resulted in postalveolar sounds.
